title: Swift Sets
content:
### Swift Sets

Sets are unordered collections of unique values. The `Set` type ensures no duplicate elements and provides fast membership testing (O(1) average).

**Creating Sets:**

```swift
// Set literal
var fruits: Set = ["Apple", "Banana", "Cherry", "Apple"]
print(fruits)  // ["Banana", "Apple", "Cherry"] (duplicate removed, order not guaranteed)

// Empty set
var emptySet = Set<Int>()
var anotherEmpty: Set<String> = []

// From array
let numbers = Set([1, 2, 3, 2, 1])  // {1, 2, 3}
```

**Set Properties and Methods:**

| Operation | Syntax | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| Count | `set.count` | Number of elements |
| Check empty | `set.isEmpty` | |
| Insert | `set.insert(5)` | Add element |
| Remove | `set.remove(5)` | Remove element |
| Contains | `set.contains(5)` | Membership test |
| Remove all | `set.removeAll()` | Clear the set |

```swift
var tags: Set = ["swift", "ios", "programming"]
tags.insert("apple")
tags.insert("swift")  // No effect, already present

if tags.contains("swift") {
    print("Swift tag found")
}

tags.remove("ios")
print(tags)  // ["swift", "programming", "apple"]
```

**When to Use Sets vs Arrays:**

| Use Case | Set | Array |
|----------|-----|-------|
| Unique elements | Yes | No guarantees |
| Ordered | No | Yes |
| Fast membership test | O(1) | O(n) |
| Random access by index | No | O(1) |

> **Key Point:** Sets are automatically hashable if their elements conform to `Hashable`. All basic Swift types (Int, String, Double, Bool) are Hashable.

Sets are optimized for fast lookup and uniqueness checks.

quiz.question: Can a Set contain duplicate elements?
What is the time complexity of contains() on a Set?
